extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian won the Stage 4 ITT with a 3km steep climb finish, taking the race lead from JEANNESSON Arnold by 1:31 over CONTADOR Alberto.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step positioned ALAPHILIPPE in front echelons Stages 1-3, forcing GC rivals to chase repeatedly before decisive ITT.
CONTADOR Alberto lost critical time in Stage 1 crosswinds, draining energy reserves before the summit-finish ITT favored climbers.
3km steep ITT finish eliminated sprinter's jersey defense; GC shakeout occurred before mountain stages via climbing time trial.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: dominated climbing ITT over pure climbers after Quick-Step protected him through three crosswind stages
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: neutral: 1:31 deficit despite strong ITT suggests Stage 1 echelon chase compromised peak form
rider_signals.JEANNESSON Arnold: negative: lost leader's jersey on steep ITT finish, confirming sprinters cannot defend GC through climbing tests
